chapter: JEFP109 – The Book That Saved the Earth
title: The Book That Saved the Earth – Medium SAQ

questions.question: Why is the twentieth century described as the ‘Era of the Book’?
questions.answer: The twentieth century is called the ‘Era of the Book’ because books were the main source of knowledge, education, and information.

questions.question: Describe Think-Tank’s attitude towards Earth.
questions.answer: Think-Tank considers Earth primitive and inferior, showing his arrogance and ignorance.

questions.question: Why does Think-Tank frequently look into the mirror?
questions.answer: He looks into the mirror to admire himself, showing his vanity and self-importance.

questions.question: Why do the Martians misunderstand the purpose of books?
questions.answer: The Martians misunderstand books because they are unfamiliar with Earth’s culture and reading habits.

questions.question: Explain the role of Captain Omega in the mission.
questions.answer: Captain Omega leads the Martian mission on Earth and reports their findings to Think-Tank.

questions.question: Why do the Martians believe books are sandwiches?
questions.answer: They believe books are sandwiches because they see pictures and lack understanding of reading.

questions.question: How do nursery rhymes create fear among the Martians?
questions.answer: The Martians take the figurative language in nursery rhymes literally and assume Earth has dangerous powers.

questions.question: What misunderstanding arises from the rhyme ‘Mistress Mary’?
questions.answer: Think-Tank believes Earthlings can grow valuable metals like silver.

questions.question: Why does Think-Tank feel threatened after ‘Hey Diddle Diddle’?
questions.answer: He believes animals on Earth possess extraordinary and dangerous abilities.

questions.question: Why does Think-Tank relate the Humpty Dumpty rhyme to himself?
questions.answer: His insecurity and arrogance make him believe the rhyme predicts his downfall.

questions.question: What decision does Think-Tank take after reading the rhymes?
questions.answer: He decides to cancel the invasion of Earth.

questions.question: Why do the Martians flee towards Alpha Centauri?
questions.answer: They flee due to fear created by misunderstanding Earth’s knowledge.

questions.question: How does Noodle differ from Think-Tank?
questions.answer: Noodle is thoughtful and willing to learn, unlike the arrogant Think-Tank.

questions.question: Why is Noodle chosen as the new leader of Mars?
questions.answer: Noodle is intelligent, practical, and open to learning.

questions.question: What role does the Historian play in the drama?
questions.answer: The Historian provides background, narration, and future perspective.

questions.question: How does humour enhance the play?
questions.answer: Humour makes the criticism of ignorance light and engaging.

questions.question: What does the library symbolise in the play?
questions.answer: The library symbolises accumulated human knowledge.

questions.question: Why is the title of the play appropriate?
questions.answer: Because a simple book prevents a powerful alien invasion.

questions.question: What message does the play convey about knowledge?
questions.answer: Knowledge is more powerful than weapons or force.

questions.question: Why do Earthlings later teach Martians to read?
questions.answer: To remove ignorance and establish peaceful relations.

questions.question: What genre does the play belong to?
questions.answer: The play belongs to science fiction comedy.

questions.question: How does fear influence Think-Tank’s decisions?
questions.answer: Fear makes him take hasty and wrong decisions.

questions.question: What flaw leads to Think-Tank’s downfall?
questions.answer: His arrogance and false sense of intelligence.

questions.question: Why is the play still relevant today?
questions.answer: Because ignorance and misunderstanding still cause conflict.

questions.question: What lesson does the play teach leaders?
questions.answer: True leadership requires humility and willingness to learn.
